_README: Illustrative seed vocabulary for the Concord demo. These codes cover ONLY the entities present in the 5 demo documents so reconciliation/coding is fast and accurate on stage. RxNorm/LOINC/SNOMED/ICD-10 values are real-world plausible and the well-known ones (HbA1c 4548-4, creatinine 2160-0, T2DM E11.9/44054006, penicillin allergy 91936005) are correct; verify any code against the live RxNav (RxNorm), LOINC, and UMLS APIs before relying on it in production. NDCs are correctly formatted but illustrative.

patient_identity.matching_note: No universal patient identifier. Five different MRNs + a surname spelling variant (Gonzalez/Gonzales) is the real-world entity-resolution / patient-matching problem (Fellegi-Sunter probabilistic linkage). Match on DOB + name similarity + demographics.

medications.sig: 1 tablet PO twice daily with meals

medications.reconciliation_flag: DUPLICATE-THERAPY RISK: PCP 'Lisinopril' and Cardiology 'Zestril' are the SAME drug (brand vs generic). A naive system treats them as two drugs.

medications.canonical_display: Aspirin 81 MG Delayed Release Oral Tablet

medications.sig: 1 tablet PO every 12 hours x 7 days (acute course, started 2026-05-22)

labs.unit_normalization: IFCC mmol/mol -> NGSP %: pct = (0.09148 * mmol_mol) + 2.152. (52 mmol/mol ~= 6.9%).
labs.trend: RISING 6.1 -> 6.5 -> 6.9% over ~4 months. Requires unit normalization to even see the trend.
RISING and above goal (<100) despite simvastatin -> statin not at goal.
DECLINING into CKD G3a range (45-59). Relevant to metformin safety + lisinopril/HCTZ + potassium.

conditions.note: inferred from eGFR 58 trend; NOT yet coded by any provider -> potential care gap

allergies.semantic_note: Same allergy appears as free text, an abbreviation, and is MISSING from cardiology + pharmacy. A reconciled record must carry it everywhere.

expected_insights.title: Clarithromycin + Simvastatin -- contraindicated combination
expected_insights.why: Clarithromycin (CYP3A4 inhibitor) markedly raises simvastatin levels -> risk of myopathy/rhabdomyolysis. Label-contraindicated.
expected_insights.cross_provider: Urgent care (QuickCare, 05/22) prescribed clarithromycin WITHOUT the med list; simvastatin was prescribed by PCP. No single system saw both.

expected_insights.title: Lisinopril and Zestril are the same drug
expected_insights.why: PCP lists generic 'Lisinopril 10 mg'; cardiology lists brand 'Zestril 10 mg'. Treated as separate, the patient risks a double dose of one ACE inhibitor.

expected_insights.title: Worsening glycemic control (A1c 6.1 -> 6.5 -> 6.9%)
expected_insights.why: Only visible after normalizing the most recent result from IFCC mmol/mol (52) to NGSP % (6.9). Suggests treatment intensification discussion.

expected_insights.title: Declining kidney function (eGFR >60 -> 58; creatinine 0.9 -> 1.2)
expected_insights.why: Emerging CKD G3a affects metformin safety and ACEi/thiazide/potassium management. Not yet acknowledged in any provider's problem list.

expected_insights.title: LDL above goal on simvastatin (138 -> 152 mg/dL)
expected_insights.why: LDL rising and >100 despite therapy -> statin not at goal.

expected_insights.title: Overdue diabetic retinal eye exam
expected_insights.why: Recommended annually for diabetes; none on file per PCP health-maintenance section.

expected_insights.title: Overdue colorectal cancer screening
expected_insights.why: USPSTF recommends screening ages 45-75; none on file. Patient is 58.

expected_insights.title: Missing urine albumin-creatinine ratio (diabetic kidney screen)
expected_insights.why: Recommended for diabetic kidney monitoring, especially with declining eGFR; not on file.
